Module 1 · Learn

The Six Future Tenses

Each tense has an animated timeline that shows its meaning, examples with audio 🔊, a worked example step by step, and a mini-check. Don't memorize: understand.

The classic dilemma

"will" vs "going to"

Module 2 · Practice

Smart Practice

The engine mixes all tenses (interleaving), gives you feedback that explains the rule, and saves your mistakes to bring them back in Review.

Module 3 · Memorize

Flashcards

Leitner-style spaced repetition: if you know it, you'll see it less; if you struggle, it'll come back soon. Tap the card to flip it.

Module 4 · The Big Section

The Three Conditionals

A conditional has the if clause (condition) and the main clause (result). If if comes first, use a comma. Use the Conditional Machine to see them in action.

🔧 Conditional Machine

Choose how real the situation is and see which conditional is formed.

🛠️ Build the Sentence (First Conditional)

Tap the words in the correct order.

Tap the words above…
Module 5 · Produce

Writing Studio

Here you write. The reviewer detects if you used the correct structure and gives you hints. There are also tasks for your notebook.

📓 For Your Notebook

Task 1 — My Future6 sentences: 2 with "will", 2 with "going to", 2 with Future Perfect ("By 2035, I will have…").
Task 2 — PredictionsA 60–80 word paragraph about technology in 20 years. Use 3 different future tenses.
Task 3 — Conditional Chain3 zero, 3 first, and 3 second conditional sentences about your school. Underline the "if" clause.
Task 4 — Hypothetical StoryA mini-story (8 lines) starting with "If I could travel anywhere…" (Second Conditional).
Module 6 · The Big Challenge!

Final Exam Practice

Questions that combine everything. When you finish, you'll get your score and a skill diagnosis that tells you exactly what to review.

Exam · Futures + Conditionals

Read carefully. Only one answer is correct. Judah is crossing his fingers for you!
